introRows.html: The basic systems outline the essentials of movement, firing, and hand-to-hand combat, but the true depth of StarCraft: The Miniatures Game lies in the complex systems built upon these fundamental ones. This section will introduce Special Abilities (Active, Passive, and Reaction), the Tactical and Faction Card systems, which give every army a unique personality, and the resources needed to unleash powerful abilities in crucial moments.
introRows.plain: The basic systems outline the essentials of movement, firing, and hand-to-hand combat, but the true depth of StarCraft: The Miniatures Game lies in the complex systems built upon these fundamental ones. This section will introduce Special Abilities (Active, Passive, and Reaction), the Tactical and Faction Card systems, which give every army a unique personality, and the resources needed to unleash powerful abilities in crucial moments.

rows.html: All Special Abilitie s follow the same structure and universal rules. Each ability on a Unit Card or Tactical Card is presented using a consistent format:
rows.plain: All Special Abilitie s follow the same structure and universal rules. Each ability on a Unit Card or Tactical Card is presented using a consistent format:

rows.html: A Name identifying the ability.
rows.plain: A Name identifying the ability.

rows.html: A Phase Limitation restricting when it can be used (if applicable).
rows.plain: A Phase Limitation restricting when it can be used (if applicable).

rows.html: A Type declaring whether it is Passive, Active, or Reaction.
rows.plain: A Type declaring whether it is Passive, Active, or Reaction.

rows.html: A Cost specifying the resource payment required in CP, BM, or PE (if any), and the Instruction text describing exactly what occurs when the ability resolves.
rows.plain: A Cost specifying the resource payment required in CP, BM, or PE (if any), and the Instruction text describing exactly what occurs when the ability resolves.

rows.html: An ability may be used only when all its requirements are met.
rows.plain: An ability may be used only when all its requirements are met.

rows.html: The timing or trigger condition must be correct, and any Phase restrictions must be satisfied.
rows.plain: The timing or trigger condition must be correct, and any Phase restrictions must be satisfied.

rows.html: The target must be Within Range and Line of Sight (unless the ability explicitly states otherwise).
rows.plain: The target must be Within Range and Line of Sight (unless the ability explicitly states otherwise).

rows.html: All Costs must be paid in full before the ability resolves, and the Unit using the ability must be on the battlefield.
rows.plain: All Costs must be paid in full before the ability resolves, and the Unit using the ability must be on the battlefield.

rows.html: Unless stated otherwise, all effects granted by Active or Reaction Abilities that expire " at the End of the Round" remain active until all End of the Round effects (Part 8. 9. 4) are resolved. They are removed during Cleanup & Refresh (Part 8. 9. 5).
rows.plain: Unless stated otherwise, all effects granted by Active or Reaction Abilities that expire " at the End of the Round" remain active until all End of the Round effects (Part 8. 9. 4 ) are resolved. They are removed during Cleanup & Refresh (Part 8. 9. 5 ).

rows.html: Active Abilities represent deliberate manoeuvres, technologies or enhancements that require a command to execute. A Unit may only use an Active Ability when it is currently Activated.
rows.plain: Active Abilities represent deliberate manoeuvres, technologies or enhancements that require a command to execute. A Unit may only use an Active Ability when it is currently Activated .

rows.html: The ability must be triggered either immediately before declaring an action or immediately after fully resolving one.
rows.plain: The ability must be triggered either immediately before declaring an action or immediately after fully resolving one.

rows.html: It cannot be used during an action.
rows.plain: It cannot be used during an action.

rows.html: Active Abilitiescannot be used while the Unit is in Reserves unless the ability explicitly states otherwise.
rows.plain: Active Abilities cannot be used while the Unit is in Reserves unless the ability explicitly states otherwise.

rows.html: Unless stated otherwise, modifiers and effects granted by an Active Ability expire at the End of the Current Round.
rows.plain: Unless stated otherwise, modifiers and effects granted by an Active Ability expire at the End of the Current Round.

rows.html: Once Per Round: A specific Unit may use a named Active Ability only once per Game Round, unless the ability has the REPEATABLE Keyword. This limit applies to each Unit individually. Two different squads of the same Unit type can both use the same-named ability in the same Round, but a single squad may not use it twice. If a Unit gains access to an Active Ability via a Tactical Card, the Once Per Round limit still applies, regardless of how many copies of that card the player controls.
rows.plain: Once Per Round: A specific Unit may use a named Active Ability only once per Game Round, unless the ability has the REPEATABLE Keyword. This limit applies to each Unit individually. Two different squads of the same Unit type can both use the same-named ability in the same Round, but a single squad may not use it twice. If a Unit gains access to an Active Ability via a Tactical Card , the Once Per Round limit still applies, regardless of how many copies of that card the player controls.

rows.html: Effects granted by Active Abilities that expire at the End of the Round remain active through the resolution of all End of the Round effects (Part 8. 9. 4). They are removed during Cleanup & Refresh (Part 8. 9. 5).
rows.plain: Effects granted by Active Abilities that expire at the End of the Round remain active through the resolution of all End of the Round effects (Part 8. 9. 4 ). They are removed during Cleanup & Refresh (Part 8. 9. 5 ).

rows.html: Passive Abilities represent innate traits, permanent equipment or constant biological adaptations.
rows.plain: Passive Abilities represent innate traits, permanent equipment or constant biological adaptations.

rows.html: They are always active as long as the Unit is on the battlefield and do not require the Unit to be Activated to function.
rows.plain: They are always active as long as the Unit is on the battlefield and do not require the Unit to be Activated to function.

rows.html: Like Active Abilities, Passive Abilities on a Unit are not in effect while that Unit is in Reserves, unless the ability explicitly states otherwise.
rows.plain: Like Active Abilities , Passive Abilities on a Unit are not in effect while that Unit is in Reserves , unless the ability explicitly states otherwise.

rows.html: Priority: If multiple Passive Abilities trigger simultaneously, the controlling player chooses the order in which they resolve. If both players have Passive Abilities triggering at the same time, the Active Player resolves theirs first.
rows.plain: Priority: If multiple Passive Abilities trigger simultaneously, the controlling player chooses the order in which they resolve. If both players have Passive Abilities triggering at the same time, the Active Player resolves theirs first.

rows.html: Reaction Abilities allow a player to act outside the usual activation sequence, responding to specific events as they occur. A Reaction is used in direct response to a defined trigger (for example, " When this Unit is targeted by an attack" ).
rows.plain: Reaction Abilities allow a player to act outside the usual activation sequence, responding to specific events as they occur. A Reaction is used in direct response to a defined trigger (for example, " When this Unit is targeted by an attack" ).

rows.html: It must be declared at the exact moment the trigger occurs.
rows.plain: It must be declared at the exact moment the trigger occurs.

rows.html: If the window is missed, the ability cannot be used retroactively.
rows.plain: If the window is missed, the ability cannot be used retroactively.

rows.html: Like Active Abilities, Reaction Abilitiescannot be used while the Unit is in Reserves unless stated otherwise.
rows.plain: Like Active Abilities , Reaction Abilities cannot be used while the Unit is in Reserves unless stated otherwise.

rows.html: Unless stated otherwise, modifiers and effects granted by a Reaction Ability expire at the End of the Current Round.
rows.plain: Unless stated otherwise, modifiers and effects granted by a Reaction Ability expire at the End of the Current Round.

rows.html: Frequency Limit: A specific Unit may use a named Reaction Ability only once per Game Round. This follows the same rules as the Active Ability frequency limit described in Part 10. 2.
rows.plain: Frequency Limit: A specific Unit may use a named Reaction Ability only once per Game Round. This follows the same rules as the Active Ability frequency limit described in Part 10. 2 .

rows.html: Reaction Limit: Each player may resolve only one Reaction per each Activation. If both players wish to use a Reaction in response to the same trigger, the Active Player resolves their Reaction first, and the opponent resolves theirs second.
rows.plain: Reaction Limit: Each player may resolve only one Reaction per each Activation. If both players wish to use a Reaction in response to the same trigger, the Active Player resolves their Reaction first, and the opponent resolves theirs second.

rows.html:
EXAMPLE: If a Zealot Unit is attacked. It may use Zealous Round OR Ground Armour, not both in the same Enemy Activation.
If a Marine Unit is attacked, and it uses Infantry Armor for its Armour Roll, the Medic Unit behind will not be able to use its Life Support in the same Enemy Activation.
rows.plain: EXAMPLE: If a Zealot Unit is attacked. It may use Zealous Round OR Ground Armour , not both in the same Enemy Activation . If a Marine Unit is attacked, and it uses Infantry Armor for its Armour Roll, the Medic Unit behind will not be able to use its Life Support in the same Enemy Activation .

rows.html: An army isn’t just men and guns; it’s doctrine, infrastructure, and command structure that keep everything in line. The Faction Cards outline what you are, what philosophy you adhere to, and what command structure your men follow. The Tactical Cards represent the facilities, technology, and strategic planning done before the first bullet is ever fired. Together, they define not just what you can bring to the battlefield, but how you can deploy it.
rows.plain: An army isn’t just men and guns; it’s doctrine, infrastructure, and command structure that keep everything in line. The Faction Cards outline what you are, what philosophy you adhere to, and what command structure your men follow. The Tactical Cards represent the facilities, technology, and strategic planning done before the first bullet is ever fired. Together, they define not just what you can bring to the battlefield, but how you can deploy it.

rows.html: Each card is presented with a standard layout. It displays a Card Name. The card specifies its Type (either Faction Card or Tactical Card), lists the Resources it provides in CP, BM, or PE when Exhausted, and includes any Special Abilities that are resolved using the standard rules described in Parts 10. 1 through 10. 4.
rows.plain: Each card is presented with a standard layout. It displays a Card Name . The card specifies its Type (either Faction Card or Tactical Card ), lists the Resources it provides in CP, BM, or PE when Exhausted , and includes any Special Abilities that are resolved using the standard rules described in Parts 10. 1 through 10. 4 .

rows.html: If a card is marked as Unique, only a single copy is allowed (Part 9. 1. 5).
rows.plain: If a card is marked as Unique , only a single copy is allowed (Part 9. 1. 5 ).

rows.html: Each faction has a separate resource type to spend on the faction’s abilities: Terran uses CP, Zerg uses BM, and Protoss uses PE. These resources are provided by Exhausting cards.
rows.plain: Each faction has a separate resource type to spend on the faction’s abilities: Terran uses CP , Zerg uses BM , and Protoss uses PE . These resources are provided by Exhausting cards.

rows.html: Exhausting a Card: To pay for an ability’s cost, such as " Cost: 1 CP, " the player must Exhaust a Ready card that matches the resource type of the cost, such as a CP card. Exhausting a card means flipping it face down, after which it is regarded as Exhausted.
rows.plain: Exhausting a Card : To pay for an ability’s cost, such as " Cost: 1 CP, " the player must Exhaust a Ready card that matches the resource type of the cost, such as a CP card. Exhausting a card means flipping it face down, after which it is regarded as Exhausted .

rows.html: If the player cannot Exhaust enough cards to pay the entire cost, they cannot activate the ability. If a card generates more resources than are needed to pay for a cost, any excess resources are lost. Resources generated to activate one ability can not be saved or spent on another ability.
rows.plain: If the player cannot Exhaust enough cards to pay the entire cost, they cannot activate the ability. If a card generates more resources than are needed to pay for a cost, any excess resources are lost. Resources generated to activate one ability can not be saved or spent on another ability.

rows.html: Every Tactical Card and Faction Card is in one of two states: Ready (face-up) or Exhausted (face-down). A Ready card may be Exhausted in two ways: the player Exhausts it to use a Special Ability printed directly on the card, or the player Exhausts it to generate its Resource in order to pay for an ability located elsewhere (for example, on a Unit Card).
rows.plain: Every Tactical Card and Faction Card is in one of two states: Ready (face-up) or Exhausted (face-down). A Ready card may be Exhausted in two ways: the player Exhausts it to use a Special Ability printed directly on the card, or the player Exhausts it to generate its Resource in order to pay for an ability located elsewhere (for example, on a Unit Card ).

rows.html: Once a card is Exhausted, it provides no resources for the rest of the round, and its abilities cannot be used until it is Refreshed (flipped face-up) during Phase 4 Cleanup.
rows.plain: Once a card is Exhausted, it provides no resources for the rest of the round, and its abilities cannot be used until it is Refreshed (flipped face-up) during Phase 4 Cleanup.
